name: URACHAL CARCINOMA
definition: rare tumor arising from the urachus (= vestigial remnant of cloaca + allantois) within space of
distribution: often peripheral curvilinear / punctate stippled psammomatous calcifications (50-72%)
discriminating_features: midline mass anterosuperior to vesical dome in the space of Retzius with predominantly
tumor bulk outside bladder + invasion of bladder dome (88%)
often peripheral curvilinear / punctate stippled psammomatous calcifications (50-72%)

general: midline mass anterosuperior to vesical dome in the space of Retzius with predominantly
tumor bulk outside bladder + invasion of bladder dome (88%)
often peripheral curvilinear / punctate stippled psammomatous calcifications (50-72%)
US: midline fluid-filled cavity with mixed echogenicity + calcifications adjacent to anterior
MR: markedly increased SI on T2WI ← mucin

clinical.Cx: pseudomyxoma peritonei (in peritoneal carcinomatosis); metastasis to pelvic lymph nodes,
clinical.Rx: radical cystectomy; often local recurrence within 2 years
